title: Biomimicry
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Biomimicry is (0) the practice of learning from and mimicking nature's strategies to solve human design challenges. This innovative approach has led to numerous breakthroughs in technology and engineering.||Nature has spent millions of years perfecting solutions (9) to complex problems through evolution. By studying these natural designs, scientists and engineers can create more efficient and sustainable technologies. For example, the bullet train in Japan was designed (10) after studying how kingfisher birds dive into water without creating a splash. This biomimetic design reduced noise and increased the train's speed.||Another fascinating example is Velcro, (11) which was invented after a Swiss engineer noticed how burrs stuck to his dog's fur. The lotus leaf (12) has inspired self-cleaning surfaces because water droplets pick up dirt as they roll (13) off the leaf's waxy surface. Shark skin has influenced swimsuit design, (14) while termite mounds have provided insights into building ventilation systems that don't require air conditioning.||As we face environmental challenges, biomimicry offers (15) a promising approach to developing sustainable solutions. (16) By learning from nature's time-tested patterns, we can create technologies that work with natural systems rather than against them.

example.text: Biomimicry is (0) practice of learning from and mimicking nature's strategies.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'solutions to problems' indica respuestas a desafíos.
Preposición 'after' indica que el diseño siguió el estudio de las aves.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere a Velcro.
Presente perfecto: 'has inspired' indica influencia hasta el presente.
Phrasal verb 'roll off' significa rodar y caer de una superficie.
Conjunción 'while' contrasta o añade otro ejemplo de biomimética.
Preposición 'By' introduce el método o medio para crear tecnologías.
